Wheely

A Self-Leveling Tricycle Robotic Platform

Kinematic simulation for uneven terrain
Python Three.js WebSocket FastAPI

The Platform

       [Wheel A]  (apex)
          /\
         /  \
        /    \
arm1   /      \   arm2
      /  brace \
     /====[]====\
    /            \
[Wheel B]    [Wheel C]

Three-wheel-drive, all wheels independently steered. Two parallelogram arms from apex to rear wheels, connected by a cross brace.

Key Innovation

  • Parallelogram linkages keep wheel shafts parallel
  • Platform stays level as arms adapt
  • Self-leveling is geometric, not active
  • Mid-size prototype: ~1-2m footprint

Use Cases

  • Forestry and rough ground logistics
  • Power platform for uneven terrain
  • Cargo hauling over obstacles

Interactive Simulation

Browser-based 3D visualization with real-time parameter adjustment.
WebSocket streams simulation frames at ~30fps.

Terrain Adaptation

IK Solver in Action

IK solver finds arm reach angles placing all wheels on the terrain surface.

Arm
0.92m
Splay
30°
Brace
0.67
Wheel
0.14m

6 Terrain Presets

  • Flat, Gentle/Steep/Cross Slope
  • Bumpy (sinusoidal), Rough (mixed)

Figure-8 Motion Simulation

Lemniscate path on bumpy terrain with passive dynamics.
Arms adapt reach angles in real time. Stability margin and tilt tracked continuously.

Python Architecture

geometry.py

PlatformConfig dataclass with all tunable dimensions. Computes wheel positions, brace endpoints, tilt rotation matrices.

terrain.py

Pluggable terrain: Flat, Slope, Sinusoidal, Composed. All implement height(x,y) and normal(x,y).

kinematics.py

Forward & inverse kinematics. IK uses scipy.optimize to solve arm reach angles. Stability margin via support triangle.

dynamics.py

Semi-implicit Euler integration. Gravity torques, contact penalty forces, hard surface clamping, pluggable actuation strategies.

NumPy SciPy FastAPI WebSocket pytest

Simulation Approach

Degrees of Freedom

  • tilt_pitch — forward/backward
  • tilt_roll — left/right
  • reach_b, reach_c — arm extension
  • Parallelogram: all shafts parallel

Physics Pipeline

  1. Apply prescribed motion
  2. Compute strategy torques (4-tuple)
  3. Gravity torques on tilt axes
  4. Terrain contact torques per arm
  5. Semi-implicit Euler integration
  6. Hard clamp: reach → tilt adjustment

Actuation Strategies

Passive

Zero torque. Arms settle via gravity + contact.

Active Arms (PD)

PD on both tilt axes. Controls pitch and roll.

Active Brace (PD)

Brace actuator. Roll direct, pitch indirect.

Spring-Damper

Spring return + damping on arm reaches.

Key Code: Dynamics Step

def simulate_step(state, config, terrain, strategy,
                   dt=0.01, motion="stationary"):
    if motion == "figure8":
        state = figure8_motion(state, dt)       # lemniscate path

    torques = strategy.compute_torques(state)    # (pitch, roll, b, c)

    # Gravity torques on tilt axes
    grav_pitch = -M * G * L_COG * sin(state.tilt_pitch)
    grav_roll  = -M * G * L_COG * sin(state.tilt_roll)

    # Terrain contact per arm (penalty spring + damping)
    contact_b = _compute_contact_for_arm(config, terrain, state, "B")
    contact_c = _compute_contact_for_arm(config, terrain, state, "C")

    # Semi-implicit Euler → hard clamp (brentq) → return
    new_pitch_vel = pitch_vel + (net_pitch / inertia) * dt
    new_tilt_pitch = tilt_pitch + new_pitch_vel * dt
    return new_state, metrics

Key Code: Parametric Geometry

@dataclass
class PlatformConfig:
    arm_length: float = 0.8        # parallelogram arm (m)
    arm_splay_angle: float = rad(40)  # from centerline
    brace_position: float = 0.5    # cross brace attach point
    wheel_radius: float = 0.15     # wheel radius (m)
    clearance: float = 0.03        # gap above wheel (m)

def compute_wheel_positions(config, tilt_pitch, tilt_roll,
                             arm_reaches):
    R = tilt_rotation_matrix(tilt_pitch, tilt_roll)  # Ry @ Rx

    def _arm_endpoint(reach, splay_sign):
        dx = arm_length * cos(splay) * cos(reach)
        dy = arm_length * splay_sign * sin(splay) * cos(reach)
        dz = -arm_length * sin(reach)
        return R @ np.array([dx, dy, dz])

    b = origin + _arm_endpoint(reach_b, -1.0)
    c = origin + _arm_endpoint(reach_c, +1.0)
    return {"A": origin, "B": b, "C": c}

Frontend & Communication

Three.js Visualization

  • Real-time 3D scene with orbit camera
  • Platform model from parametric config
  • Terrain mesh from heightmap grid
  • Support triangle and stability overlay
  • Live metrics chart (canvas, no deps)

WebSocket Protocol

  • set_config, set_terrain, set_strategy
  • set_motion — stationary / figure-8
  • start_sim / stop_sim / solve_ik
  • Server streams frame at ~30fps

File Structure

web/
  index.html       # Layout, sliders
  simulation.js    # WS, animation loop
  scene.js         # Three.js setup
  platform-viz.js  # 3D platform
  terrain-viz.js   # Terrain mesh
  controls.js      # Slider bindings
  metrics-chart.js # Time-series chart

Tech Choices

  • Vanilla JS, ES modules + importmap
  • CSS Grid layout, dark theme
  • FastAPI serves static + WebSocket

Status & Next Steps

Working Now

  • Full parametric geometry with real-time 3D
  • 6 terrain presets (flat through rough)
  • Forward & inverse kinematics
  • 4 actuation strategies with live comparison
  • Figure-8 prescribed motion
  • Two-axis tilt dynamics, hard surface clamp
  • Stability margin tracking
  • Config save/load (JSON)

Planned

  • Driven motion: steering + speed
  • Path planning and waypoints
  • Motor selection and power budget
  • Deformable terrain (mud, sand)
  • Physical prototype construction

pip install -e ".[dev]"
uvicorn wheely.server:app --port 8000
